data.text: a feeling of great happiness or pleasure: delight
examples: Seeing you happy gives me such joy.
They were shouting/jumping for joy. =they shouted/jumped because they were very happy
a gathering full of joy and laughter
wishes of love, joy, and peace for all
The sun is the joy and the sorrow of the people. It destroys their crops while it warms them with the heat they could not live without.
Discovering something new is one of the great joys of eating out.
The old boat was his pride and joy.
The baby is a little bundle of joy.

data.text: the expression or exhibition of such emotion
examples: Seeing you happy gives me such joy.

data.text: a state of great happiness: bliss
examples: Seeing you happy gives me such joy.
They were shouting/jumping for joy. =they shouted/jumped because they were very happy
a gathering full of joy and laughter

data.text: to experience great pleasure or delight: rejoice

enrichment.etymology: Middle English, from Anglo-French joie, from Latin gaudia, plural of gaudium, from gaudēre to rejoice; probably akin to Greek gēthein to rejoice
